text.title: Load Balancer Types and Algorithm Selection Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns from real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Mobile users randomly logged out; NAT clusters all hit one backend.
antipattern.root-cause: IP hash used as affinity; client IP is not stable.
antipattern.fix: Cookie-based affinity or server-side session store (Redis).

antipattern.symptom: Need path-routing or header injection — LB cannot help.
antipattern.root-cause: L4 chosen 'to avoid HTTP parsing overhead'.
antipattern.fix: Move to L7 (HAProxy / Envoy / managed ALB).

antipattern.symptom: Some backends idle; one always saturated.

antipattern.root-cause: Consistent hash used on skewed user IDs.
antipattern.fix: Limit consistent hash to cache tier; use round-robin/least-conn for web.
